spec_match.rationale: Surfaces the two new tokens alongside the existing four and emits a syntactically correct header, but the spec's special-case (encountering `cache` or `*` auto-appends `prefetchCache`) is invisible — selecting `cache` shows nothing happening to the prefetch tier.

interactive.rationale: Real checkboxes drive a live header string and an impact table; this is meaningfully past a static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb promises a logout-flow builder that shows the assembled header and resulting state — the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path only. No cross-origin no-op, no `cache` auto-promotes-prefetchCache rule, no active-prerender skip behaviour from the spec, no demonstration of what survives (cookies/storage) vs what dies.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/4755745652080640 in the see-also block.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no static-card pattern, accent-emerald and accent-rose colours are paired with --bg-ivory text for contrast.

openQuestions.title: Show the `cache` → `prefetchCache` auto-append behaviour
openQuestions.detail: The spec says that when the parser sees `cache` or `*`, it appends `prefetchCache` to the list. Right now ticking `cache` only marks the HTTP cache tier as cleared and the new tier as untouched — the opposite of reality. Either co-highlight the prefetch row when `cache` is selected, or add a small explanatory line under the header readout that says `because cache was set, prefetchCache is also implicitly cleared`.

openQuestions.title: Add a cross-origin scenario toggle
openQuestions.detail: The spec confines clearing to the response origin. A toggle that lets the user pick whether the simulated request was same-origin or cross-origin would surface the rule that other origins' prefetches survive — currently the page implies the clear is global.

openQuestions.title: Highlight which row is new in the table, not just in the chip
openQuestions.detail: The .new class is defined for table rows but the chips above are where the `(new)` text lives. Mark the prefetchCache/prerenderCache rows visually in the impact table so the v138 deltas pop without having to read the labels.

openQuestions.title: Add the active-prerender exception
openQuestions.detail: The spec says active (currently-prerendering) traversables are skipped by the clear. The page treats `prerenderCache` as a single yes/no — surface the case where a prerender is in flight and the clear leaves it alone (or queues until activation).

summary: Honest builder that exposes the new tokens but misses the two spec subtleties that make this feature interesting: the `cache`/`*` auto-append and the active-prerender skip. Adding both — plus a cross-origin toggle — would lift this from a header-spelling demo to a real spec teaching tool.
